summary:
Intensifying screens enhance film sensitivity by emitting light upon exposure to X-rays, allowing for significantly reduced radiation dosage to the patient while maintaining image quality. This reduction in required radiation makes them primarily effective at decreasing patient exposure rather than altering image contrast directly.  

(Note: While some sources suggest possible effects on contrast, primary role remains minimizing radiation.) 

Final answer choice based on core mechanism: $\boxed{\text{Decreases radiation}}$ or equivalent meaning.

Corrected final summary focusing only on essential clinical point(s):

An intensifying screen increases film sensitivity by converting X-rays into visible light, thereby enabling lower radiation doses to be delivered without compromising diagnostic image quality. Its main purpose in extraoral radiography is to reduce radiation exposure to patients.
